title: Yamamoto Taro resigns as leader of Reiwa Shinsengumi and retires from politics
summary: The leader of the Reiwa Shinsengumi party announces his definitive retirement from politics, and the party will change its name and elect a new representative.

title: United States and Iran intensify mutual attacks and concern grows over the Strait of Hormuz
summary: Iran attacks US military infrastructure and the US expands its targets to more than 170 points, while Japan warns its citizens.

title: British singer Bonnie Tyler dies at 75
summary: Known for her hits 'Total Eclipse of the Heart' and 'Holding Out for a Hero', the husky-voiced singer leaves a musical legacy.

title: Court orders 157 million yen compensation to student who became quadriplegic in physical education class
summary: The Fukuoka court recognized the school's responsibility for the neck fracture during a vault exercise.

title: Yakult Swallows achieve a no-hit game with three foreign pitchers, first in history
summary: Foreign relievers combined for the first no-hitter of the season in the Japanese professional baseball league.

title: Debate over Imperial House Law reform intensifies in Parliament
summary: The center party abstains and there are strong objections within the ruling party over the institutionalization of adoption.

title: Tensions mark NATO summit as Trump links presence in Europe to Greenland and Iran
summary: Lack of trust among European allies and Trump's threats create uncertainty about the future of the alliance.

title: Germany accelerates its military rearmament and agrees to acquire Tomahawk missiles from the US
summary: Chancellor Merz announces the purchase of Tomahawk missiles, as the country seeks to increase its defense budget to fourth place worldwide.

title: New evidence presented to request review of the Ōsaki case
summary: Lawyers present 11 new pieces of evidence including an experiment that questions the possibility of strangulation with a towel.

title: FamilyMart opens its first flagship store with fitting rooms and own-brand clothing
summary: The new store features fitting rooms and a stand to buy fried chicken, while Lawson introduces crane machines.

title: Uproar over resale of Shonen Jump magazine extras before launch
summary: Bookstores warn about the resale of figures included as gifts, generating criticism about the lack of respect for reading.

title: Both sides appeal 30-year prison sentence for beating death of a university student in Hokkaido
summary: Both the prosecution and the accused file an appeal against the conviction for the murder of the student in Ebetsu.

title: 'Man for hire' arrested for welfare fraud linked to murder of IT executive
summary: Authorities discovered that the suspect did not declare income while receiving social assistance, as part of the murder investigation.

title: Bankruptcy of card payment provider affects restaurants and generates million-dollar losses for banks
summary: The chairman of Keidanren calls for healthy management among payment operators after the bankruptcy of Zen Toh Shin.

title: Construction of floats for Kyoto's Gion Festival begins
summary: Assembly work on the floats begins with the Naginata Hoko float leading the July 17 procession.

title: Typhoon No. 9 approaches Sakishima and Okinawa islands with extreme violence
summary: The typhoon is expected to reach the islands on July 11 with winds capable of destroying homes.

title: Deep-sea mining threatens to extinguish 62% of hydrothermal vent species
summary: The updated Red List includes the world's unique snail and other species critically endangered by marine resource exploitation.

title: Proposed law penalizes destruction of national flag for political protest
summary: The bill's author says it contributes to fostering patriotism, but controversy persists over the ambiguity of the crime.

title: Investigation reveals possible organ trafficking in Cambodian hospitals involving Japanese patients
summary: It was discovered that patients signed confidentiality agreements after undergoing paid transplants, while hospitals deny involvement.

title: Increase in fake police scams and Osaka police turn to AI to prevent them
summary: A university professor develops an AI-generated video featuring the 'AI commissioner' to explain romance and impersonation scams.
